title: Consultation on guidelines for Canadian drinking water quality – Iron: Closed consultation - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation on guidelines for Canadian drinking water quality – Iron: Closed consultation

## Current status: Closed
This consultation ran fom September 29, 2023, to November 28, 2023.
Health Canada has developed a technical document to provide regulatory authorities and decision makers with an objective for the level of iron substances in Canadian drinking water supplies. We're looking for comments on this draft document from regulatory authorities, decision makers and the public.

## Who was the focus of this consultation
- industry stakeholders
- academic communities
- non-governmental organizations
- federal, provincial and territorial regulatory and health protection agencies

## Key questions for discussion
Ideas and input were sought around 3 topics:
- the proposed objective
- the approach used to develop it
- the potential impacts of implementation
The input that was gathered through this process will be used to revise the document as needed.
- Guidelines for Canadian Drinking Water Quality: Guideline Technical Document – Iron
Water and Air Quality Bureau
Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave West Address Locator 4903B Ottawa ON K1A 0K9
